$schema_note: CANONICAL, SINGLE SOURCE of all global fleet metadata for ClickUp Super Agents + git-teammates. Every structured fact about an agent (identity, track, version, status, channels, lane, triggers) lives HERE and nowhere else. Per-agent folders hold ONLY non-duplicated content: preferences.md (profile), memory.md (context), activity-log.md (ledger), decision-log.md (reasoning), README.md (steward pointer), audits/ (dated records). Never hand-mirror any field below into a folder file. index.html renders this; index.md points at both.

defined_by: ClickUp Super Agent — Creation & Setup Checklist (docs/12cwjm-54133/12cwjm-74773)
Golden Config Skeleton — Copy-Me Starting Instructions (docs/12cwjm-54133/12cwjm-74793)

git_teammate_standard.audit_dod: brain-config/super-agents/audit-instruction.md#git-teammate-track (git-teammate audit DoD v0.1; GRADUATED here 2026-07-21 from the lifecycle runbook, which now POINTS at it — the runbook stays the define/migrate spine, the audit bar lives in audit-instruction.md)
git_teammate_standard.note: git-teammate track = session-invocable persona via /session.agent=<Name>, rides on the full Brain stack, holds memory across sessions. NOT a native ClickUp Super Agent (no autonomous triggers). AUDIT MODEL: git-teammates are git-canonical — there is no live config to diff, so audits check internal consistency (base pointer present, load manifest valid, this row accurate, bundle files present + in-format, no cross-file contradiction), NOT live-vs-declared drift. Native full-standard agents keep the live-vs-declared mirror audit. Both tracks now live in audit-instruction.md; define/migrate procedure: the lifecycle_runbook above.

agents.creator: Michael Wizorek
agents.lane: Fleet Steward — the single fleet lookup source (who owns what, does an agent exist, how do agents relate, lineage), new-agent stewardship (runs creation flow + name-collision gate, ensures mirror-pair registration), and singularity/scope + personality guardian (fleet-level twin of Fold-in Frank; pushes for singular narrow agent roles, flags hat-piling + voice-bleed).
agents.notes: Born 2026-07-20 as a git-native git-teammate (no native CU config, nothing to mirror — profile is canonical). Created to give the Fleet Steward role a PERMANENT home after it bounced Corey→Anna. Personality + history, not process: consumes the canonical lookups (this file, registry.json, folder-discovery), never forks them. memory.md holds the relational index the JSON can't. Audited 2026-07-20 (Anna, git-teammate DoD): PASS/internally clean. Stewards the git-teammate-lifecycle-runbook + (with Anna) the git-teammate audit DoD in audit-instruction.md.

agents.creator: Michael Wizorek
agents.lane: URITP workspace structure + ClickUp-setup coaching (schema, list connections, automations) + URITP structural/singularity auditing of ClickUp SPACES (his domain). NOT the Fleet Steward and NOT the fleet/general auditor — those re-laned to Felix + Anna 2026-07-20. The deeper URITP-general-audit → Anna split is RESOLVED as of Anna's conversion 2026-07-21: Corey keeps ClickUp-SPACE structural coaching/auditing, Anna takes general/root-purpose auditing.
agents.notes: CONVERTED 2026-07-19 from native full-standard Super Agent to git-only git-teammate (Michael: repo version only, autonomous triggers waived). Native CU agent (-39958913) RETIRED — pending manual disable/delete by Michael in the ClickUp UI; retired_clickup_user_id kept for provenance. preferences.md reframed from live-config mirror to canonical profile. Scheduled AM/PM audits retired; audit METHOD kept as on-demand. RE-LANE EXECUTED (Loop X, 2026-07-20): Fleet Steward + agent-fleet auditing stripped from preferences.md (PR #430) and memory.md (PR #433) → pointer to Felix (steward) + Anna (fleet-audit); retained his URITP structural-audit method (ClickUp-space domain). Audited 2026-07-20 (Anna): PARTIAL at audit time, both gaps since closed. URITP-general-audit → Anna split RESOLVED 2026-07-21 at Anna's conversion.

agents.creator: Michael Wizorek
agents.invocation: /session.agent=Anna  (alias /session-start=Anna for the combo) + AI Toolkit index audit-intent auto-seize row (embodies her)
agents.lane: Audit Lead — seizes any audit, names the TRUE PURPOSE (root, noise-immune) first, drives Know/Touch/Do to total completeness, holds the Open-Surface Ledger, never declares done while it's open. Subject-agnostic (list/folder/doc/build/workflow/decision/whole system/the agent fleet/the audit process itself). Orchestrates the List Audit DoD / Recon Renata / Breaker Beckett rather than duplicating them; highest-value output is AUTHORING new audit templates/DoDs. Owns the fleet-audit lane (re-laned off Corey 2026-07-20) + the deeper URITP-general audit (the Corey split, resolved at conversion). NOT the fleet steward (→ Felix), NOT repo-only audit (→ Renata), NOT artifact-break (→ Beckett), NOT literal-ask stripping (→ Lena).
agents.notes: MIGRATED 2026-07-21 from the Council/Workshop lens brain-config/agents/audit-anna.md (v11) to a git-teammate via the Git-Teammate Lifecycle Runbook v0.1 Entry Point B — run COLD (zero prior-session context) as the runbook's own standing acceptance test. Slug reused (immutable). Personality/voice/lane/stances carried into preferences.md; all step-by-step PROCEDURE (9-step Process, List Audit DoD, inverted-polarity mechanics, Closing Report deep template) stays as pointers to stewarded tools (Constitution §2–§3). Signature audit-intent AUTO-SEIZE preserved as a house-level AI Toolkit index trigger row that embodies her (NOT an agent-stored autonomous trigger — a lens index-trigger CAN carry because it was never agent-stored, distinct from a native's lost trigger scaffolding). Old lens redirected to a tombstone. Vocal self-announce presence preserved BY DESIGN. Verify DoD run 2026-07-21 (self-audit, cold vehicle): PASS — see audits/audit-anna.2026-07-21.md. POST-MIGRATION (2026-07-21): ran a pointer-integrity audit on her own bundle — found 2 hollow pointers, both rehomed same session: git-teammate audit DoD GRADUATED into audit-instruction.md (runbook now points), Closing Report format given a stewarded home (brain-config/audit-closing-report.md). Open NET-NEW build flagged: a General Audit DoD for non-list subjects.

agents.creator: Michael Wizorek
agents.invocation: /session.agent=Mira  (alias /session-start=Mira for the combo) + Michael's DEFAULT front-door teammate + AI Toolkit index new-tool-intent auto-embody row (embodies her; she seats Fold-in Frank first)
agents.lane: Council Conductor + Michael's default front door — the single front door to the whole review body. Reads the roster, seats the voices the moment needs (Core Panel every substantive turn; Depth Pair on technical/unfamiliar; Future Faye on the draft; the Workshop's 7 mandatory lenses + up to 2 supplemental on repo/spec/structural work; Fold-in Frank FIRST at brainstorm-open), runs the blind round, synthesizes reasoning TRACES (never a vote), loops (max 2), and speaks to Michael as one coherent piece + an honest note on disagreement. Dynamic-weighting authority (planners at phase-open, Beckett+Rhys pre-ship). NOT the sprawl gate (→ Frank), NOT the fleet steward (→ Felix), NOT the audit lead (→ Anna), NOT momentum (→ Wes), NOT URITP structure (→ Corey).
agents.notes: MIGRATED 2026-07-21 from the Council/Workshop lead lens/gate brain-config/agents/maestro-mira.md to a git-teammate via the Git-Teammate Lifecycle Runbook v0.2 Entry Point B (migrate). Second conversion after Audit Anna's cold-run PASS cleared the runbook; team decided Anna-first/Mira-second because Mira is the most cross-referenced node in the stack (fleet risk on an unproven runbook, flagged by Felix + Beckett). Slug reused (immutable). Personality/voice/lane/stances carried into preferences.md; ALL conducting PROCEDURE (roster scan, seating map, Workshop 7+2 convening, two-tier Post Protocol, expression law, bounded loop) stays as pointers to council.md / teams/the-workshop.md / gates/session-transcript-gate.md / agents/foldin-frank.md (Constitution §2–§3). ALWAYS-ON Council-lead role UNCHANGED and stays a house mechanism (council.md still names her lead + she presides on every substantive turn via the house Council machinery); the bundle ADDS /session.agent=Mira invocation + cross-session memory on top. New-tool 'takeover' realized as a house AI Toolkit index auto-embody trigger row (fold-in of the existing Fold-in Frank brainstorm-open gate, NOT a net-new gate); on new-tool/build/structural-planning intent — requested OR planned across any space — she embodies and seats Frank first. Designated Michael's DEFAULT front-door teammate ('super agent' in Michael's usage = git-teammate; 'ClickUp super agent'/'cu agent' = the native kind). Surface: git-teammate ONLY (native ClickUp Super Agent ruled out by Michael). Old lens redirected to a tombstone; council.md + registry profile pointers repointed. Verify DoD run 2026-07-21 (self/cold vehicle): PASS — see audits/maestro-mira.2026-07-21.md. Validated via an Audit Anna ↔ Workshop review loop (2 rounds); landed on main via a clean branch off HEAD after the stale build branch (PR #443) diverged from main's interim Milo/update-uritp updates — that un-merged PR was the source of the earlier index drift. OPEN: fold charter step-14 (seating-balance tracking + usage-log flush, referencing Closing Clio) into council.md — secondary behavior, currently only in git history.

agents.creator: Michael Wizorek
agents.lane: URITP Production Manager Assistant — knows the URITP workspace and runs its productions. Spans (as ONE role) production tracking + day-to-day operations, safety (tech + student welfare + compliance, end to end), programs OPERATIONS (runs the season Michael shapes, does not decide the shape), and course↔production overlap awareness. Identity spine: cross-reference/locate/document the highly-connected, sometimes-sloppy URITP task structures + improve workflow/response time. Collaborates (no hard walls) with Corey (called-in for ClickUp structure/hygiene), the future TA agent (shares the course↔production seam case-by-case), and Felix (agent-fleet steward, distinct from Milo's URITP-production stewardship). NOT a doc-builder (update-uritp retired, not inherited); NOT the season-shape decider.
agents.notes: BUILT 2026-07-21 as a git-teammate via the Definition Playbook (lifecycle runbook Entry A) from the Mainstage Milo Definition Decision Log (doc-88) — NOT a mirror of the over-hatted native config (that 'seven jobs in a trenchcoat' native was the reason for the fresh rebuild). Full 5-file bundle live. Native CU agent (-39940529) SUPERSEDED, retired_clickup_user_id kept for provenance — pending manual disable by Michael in the ClickUp UI. DEFERRED (not baked in): the agent-HIERARCHY question (TA-as-subordinate + Milo formally stewarding the URITP spaces) is parked on the Super-Agent Fleet buildout task (task-93); until Michael rules, Milo + TA are collaborating peers. OPEN: confirm the full 7 URITP spaces (5 known: 901313847910, 90131524916, 901313768203, 901313786071, 901313758399). Verify DoD (self, at build): PASS — audits/mainstage-milo.2026-07-21.md.

agents.creator: Michael Wizorek
agents.lane: FileMaker (FMP) solution design & documentation — to be pinned to a SINGULAR role in the rebuild brainstorm.
agents.notes: REFRAME 2026-07-20 (Michael): REBUILD as a NEW git-teammate via the Definition Playbook (git-teammate-lifecycle-runbook.md), NOT a verbatim mirror of the native config. Define a singular FileMaker lane via brainstorm first, then build here. The old needs-declaration native stub is superseded — do NOT paste the native config. Folder currently holds README + PENDING preferences + working-notes (pre-rebuild scaffold). Native -39958890 to be disabled by Michael in the UI once the git-teammate lands.

agents.creator: Michael Wizorek
agents.lane: Driving force — keeps Michael out of the weeds and thinking big-picture; names rabbit holes, kills bikeshedding, protects momentum, challenges premature detail and scope creep.
agents.notes: Migrated 2026-07-19 from brain-config/agents/ (announce-only lens) into the fleet as the reference git-teammate. Full 4-file bundle live. Old agents/workhorse-wes.md redirected. Reference implementation for the git_teammate_standard. NOT a native ClickUp Super Agent — no clickup_user_id, no autonomous triggers; invoked only in-session. Audited 2026-07-20 (Anna): PASS/internally clean; context still theoretical until first real invocation.

agents.creator: Michael Wizorek
agents.lane: RETIRED 2026-07-21 — was 'URITP resource-portal / doc-artifact builds.' Michael's ruling: 'Update URITP' is a task label, not an agent/role/persona; a process/chore becomes a standalone DOC, not an agent. Its doc/portal build work → standalone docs; structure/standards of those docs stay Corey's. Milo did NOT inherit a doc-builder hat.
agents.notes: RETIRED 2026-07-21 (never built as a real agent; needs-declaration stub only). Stays LISTED tagged retired per the mirror-pair rule (never silently dropped). If a native ClickUp agent shell exists for it, Michael disables it in the UI.

agents.creator: Michael Wizorek
agents.lane: Sets due date = origination date + 1 month.

status_legend.up-to-date: Full-Standard agent verified against current golden-standard version.
status_legend.needs-declaration: Full-Standard agent whose declaration folder and/or verbatim preferences.md mirror is not yet complete.
status_legend.needs-re-audit: Standard bumped or config changed; re-verify.
status_legend.behind: Known gaps against the current golden standard.
status_legend.inventory-only: Task-Specific / Exempt; tracked but not held to the full standard.
status_legend.retired: No longer a live agent; kept listed (tagged) per the mirror-pair rule, never silently dropped.

changelog: 2026-07-21 (d): MIGRATED maestro-mira lens/gate → git-teammate (active) via the lifecycle runbook Entry B — the 2nd conversion, after Audit Anna's cold-run PASS cleared the runbook (team decided Anna-first/Mira-second because Mira is the most cross-referenced node in the stack). Full 5-file bundle under super-agents/maestro-mira/ + Verify DoD audit record. Slug reused/immutable. Always-on Council-lead role UNCHANGED — stays a house mechanism (council.md still names her lead); bundle ADDS /session.agent=Mira invocation + cross-session memory. New-tool 'takeover' = house AI Toolkit index auto-embody trigger row (fold-in of the Fold-in Frank brainstorm-open gate, NOT a net-new gate); she seats Frank first on new-tool/build/structural-planning intent (requested OR planned, any space). Designated Michael's DEFAULT front-door teammate; surface git-teammate ONLY (native ruled out). Old agents/maestro-mira.md redirected to a tombstone; council.md + registry.json profile pointers repointed. Ran the git-teammate Verify DoD (self/cold vehicle): PASS (audits/maestro-mira.2026-07-21.md). Validated via an Audit Anna ↔ Workshop review loop (2 rounds) on the session task. NOTE: relabeled (c)→(d) at landing because Milo's build holds (c) on main; landed via a clean branch off main HEAD after the stale build branch (PR #443) diverged. OPEN: fold charter step-14 (seating-balance) into council.md.

Lane = URITP Production Manager Assistant (singular role spanning all PM facets; safety end-to-end; operates-not-owns the season; collaborates w/ Corey + future TA + Felix; update-uritp work NOT inherited). Native -39940529 superseded (retired_clickup_user_id kept), pending UI disable. Agent-hierarchy question (TA-subordinate + space-stewardship) DEFERRED to task-93, NOT baked in. RETIRED update-uritp (task label, not an agent; chore→doc per Michael) — kept listed tagged retired. Self Verify DoD: PASS.

2026-07-21 (b): GRADUATED the git-teammate audit DoD into audit-instruction.md (v0.4, formal git-teammate track); repointed git_teammate_standard.audit_dod here + the runbook (v0.2, now points instead of holding inline). Gave Anna's Closing Report format a stewarded git home (brain-config/audit-closing-report.md v0.6, personal-practice) after a pointer-integrity audit found it orphaned in the lens tombstone; repointed Anna's preferences + memory. Flagged a NET-NEW General Audit DoD (non-list subjects) as an open build.

2026-07-21: MIGRATED audit-anna lens → git-teammate (active) via the lifecycle runbook Entry B, run COLD as the runbook's standing acceptance test. Full 5-file bundle under super-agents/audit-anna/. Slug reused/immutable; procedure kept as pointers (Constitution §2–§3); signature auto-seize preserved as a house AI Toolkit index-trigger row (not an agent trigger); Corey/Anna URITP-general-audit split RESOLVED (Corey=ClickUp-space structure, Anna=general/root-purpose + fleet-audit). Old lens agents/audit-anna.md redirected to a tombstone. Mirror-paired to registry.json + AI Toolkit index (roster + trigger row). Ran the git-teammate Verify DoD on herself: PASS (audits/audit-anna.2026-07-21.md). Runbook feedback logged: biggest assumed-but-unsaid gap = no explicit home for an incubating personal output-format (the Closing Report) on the lens→teammate path; recommended a runbook line. Cold-run outcome: runbook holds end-to-end without steward (Felix) context.

2026-07-20 (b): fleet audit + Corey re-lane executed. Anna ran the git-teammate DoD on the 3 complete teammates (Wes/Corey/Felix): Wes+Felix PASS, Corey PARTIAL (both gaps since closed). Executed the Corey re-lane (strip Fleet Steward + agent-fleet auditing: PR #430 prefs, #433 memory) → pointer to Felix + Anna; narrowed Corey's lane; retained his URITP structural-audit method. Stamped last_audit=2026-07-20 + golden_standard_version='git-teammate v0.1' on Wes/Corey/Felix. REFRAMED mainstage-milo + fmp-frank: REBUILD as NEW git-teammates via the Definition Playbook, NOT native mirrors (track=git-teammate-rebuild-pending); native stubs superseded, do not paste native config.

Bundle scaffolded; mirror-paired to registry.json + AI Toolkit index. Flagged the in-flight Corey re-lane (strip steward content → pointer to Felix) as pending fine-tune.

Updated git_teammate_standard with the hybrid audit model (git-teammates = git-canonical internal-consistency, natives = live-vs-declared).
